title: Hirog

{{Quote|Let's hear it for the glorious destiny of the Hiromi! Hooray!|First Officer Hirog|<ref name="Zeltros: Pleasure Planet">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20040105zeltros|text=Zeltros: Pleasure Planet|int=Zeltros: Pleasure Planet}}</ref>}}
'''Hirog''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Hiromi]], and [[Executive officer/Legends|First Officer]] of the [[Hiromi Empire]] [[Flagship/Legends|mothership]] ''[[Kuratcha]]''. He proved to be an extremely unlucky spy and saboteur as he tried to expedite the Hiromi dream of galactic conquest.

==Biography==
{{Quote|I have arranged it all…by a series of mysterious, unsigned messages, an appeal to chivalry, and a swift masterful strike in a lonely spot, at an auspicious time…|Hirog reports on his efforts to unbalance the Alliance|<ref name="M94" />}}
Working as an aide of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Ackbar]] of the [[Alliance of Free Planets]], he tried to undermine their stability by starting a war between two member species, the [[Lahsbee/Huhk|Lahsbee]] and the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]]. He managed to implicate a Lahsbane delegate in improper behavior with the Ewok [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Kamida]], ensuring the [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] was there to observe it. When, as predicted, the droid reported what he had seen, [[Tippet/Legends|Tippet]], a member of the [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]] betrothed to the Princess, declared war against the Lashbees.<ref name="M94" />

[[File:Hirog-bomb.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Hirog prepares his bomb.]]

While the situation escalated, Hirog also observed the strange phenomenon of the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]], in case they should pose a hidden threat to the Hiromi Empire. Watching [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] train with some Lahsbee, Hirog was knocked out by a [[Telekinesis/Legends|Force-deflected]] rock. This encounter produced a healthy fear and respect for Jedi abilities, which would prove useful later.<ref name="M94" />

Continuing with his plans, Hirog waited until word of the Ewok-Lahsbane conflict had spread through the Alliance forces, then constructed a bomb. Aiming to kill the Alliance leadership, Hirog would ensure the blame fall on the Ewoks and Lahsbane, creating a spilt in the Alliance.<ref name="M94" />

His attempt backfired when, in the midst of an honor duel between the Lahsbee and Tippet, a stray [[Blaster/Legends|blasterbolt]] activated the bomb before the timer was set. Fleeing the undergrowth, Hirog drew the attention of the enraged Lahsbee—now in [[Lahsbee/Huhk|Huhk]] form—who threw a rock after him, crushing the bomb. It saved Hirog's life but foiled his plan. Hearing Hirog's screams of panic on the surface, and the roars of the Huhk, the Hiromi mothership ''[[Kuratcha]]'' subsequently abandoned him on Endor.<ref name="M94" />

===Operation: Glorious Destiny===
{{Quote|It sounds as if someone is at the door, and I need to hide. Hirog out.|Hirog reports to his superiors|<ref name="Zeltros: Pleasure Planet" />}}
Making his way back to the Hiromi forces, Hirog was given a second chance. Not to be deterred from their destiny, the Hiromi had continued with their plans of galactic domination, invading [[Zeltros/Legends|Zeltros]] as part of [[Operation: Glorious Destiny]]. Once again, First Officer Hirog was on the frontlines during the [[Battle of Zeltros]], this time joined by a Hiromi squad including [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Hookyr]] and [[Ensign/Legends|Ensigns]] [[Phoebe]] and [[Tahkay]]. Ensign Phoebe was smuggled into the Zeltros capital and tasked with subduing [[Arno (Zeltron)|Arno]] and [[Leonie]], the [[King/Legends|King]] and [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] of Zeltros. Phoebe kept in contact with Hirog as she prepared to carry out her mission.<ref name="M104">[[Star Wars (1977) 104|''Star Wars'' (1977) 104]]</ref>

Unfortunately for Hirog and the Hiromi Empire, their plan caught a little snag. The [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Luke Skywalker overheard their plan and made an effort to question Hirog about his conversation with Phoebe. Captain Hookyr's quick thinking saved the day as he pressed a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] into Skywalker's back and took him prisoner. The [[Hoojib/Legends|Hoojib]] [[Plif/Legends|Plif]] was also taken prisoner alongside the Jedi. Their plan now back on track, Hirog contacted Phoebe once again, to confirm that the Zeltron leaders had been subdued. Phoebe confirmed the King and Queen's incapacitation (they had drunk themselves into an incoherent stupor), and the Hiromi initiated the next phase of their plan.<ref name="M104" />

[[File:BattleofZeltros.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The [[Hiromi]], with [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and several [[Hoojib/Legends|Hoojibs]], attack the [[Tof/Legends|Tof]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]].]]

Infiltrating the Zeltron Palace, their force managed to capture the throne room—while no one else was around—placing Captain Hookyr the First on the throne. Continuing with their plans, they then secured the Royal Kitchens and proceeded to prepare a feast of sandwiches to celebrate their victory.<ref name="M104" /> However, the subsequent invasion by the [[Nagai/Legends|Nagai]], and later the [[Tof/Legends|Tofs]], foiled the Hiromi plans. A Tof invasion force led by an [[Unidentified Tof Supreme|unidentified]] [[Supreme]] stormed the Royal Kitchen and began to brutally interrogate Hirog. Luckily for the Hiromi, their "captive" Jedi was able to save the day, killing the Tof leadership and freeing the Hiromi. Hirog soon struck an important peace deal with Alliance representative Luke Skywalker, and the Hiromi joined forces with the Alliance to defeat the Tof invasion force.<ref name="M105">[[Star Wars (1977) 105|''Star Wars'' (1977) 105]]</ref> Hirog and his soldiers then helped to storm the [[Wayfarer (Tof starship)|''Wayfarer'']] and rescue the four captured [[Zeltron/Legends|Zeltron]] aides.<ref name="M106" />

With the successful end to the Battle of Zeltros,<ref name="M106" /> and a peace treaty with the Alliance, Hirog requested a transfer for himself and the entire crew of the ''Kuratcha'' to return to the hidden Hiromi homeworld, rather than continue in the upcoming [[Battle of Saijo|war]] against the Tofs.
